obligation_text: Before placing on the market or putting into service a high-risk AI system, the provider or, where applicable, the authorised representative shall register themselves and their system in the EU database referred to in Article 71.
scoring_text: Knowledge of EU AI database registration requirements, including provider registration procedures, system registration data requirements, and pre-market registration compliance.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of EU AI database registration requirements, including provider registration procedures, system registration data requirements, and pre-market registration compliance.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI regulatory requirements, including the EU AI Act prohibited use categories, high-risk system obligations, conformity assessment requirements, and technical documentation standards.
comments: EU AI Act Art. 49(1) (obligation) superset of WIDAI AG domain. AG-K-013 is the strongest match (STS: 0.552, cosine: 0.725). Secondary: AG-K-008 (AG, cosine: 0.714), RC-T-010 (RC, cosine: 0.674).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the regulatory requirement) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.5/10 (raw 0.552). Shared vocabulary: [ai, eu, knowledge, requirements, system]. EU AI Act competency implication terms [compliance, data, database, market, pre, procedures] vs WIDAI terms [act, assessment, categories, conformity, documentation, high] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.918 (P=0.923, R=0.912). Balanced P/R — the 19-word competency implication and 23-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs (mean ~0.007) because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (77.0%). Semantic tension between "Knowledge of EU AI database registration requirements, including provider regist" and "Knowledge of AI regulatory requirements, including the EU AI Act prohibited use". Despite STS-based 'Superset of' classification, these concepts may operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.725 > STS 0.552 (gap +0.172). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ai, eu, knowledge, requirements, system] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 26.7%. Shared tokens: [ai, eu, knowledge, requirements, system]. EU AI Act-only: [compliance, data, database, market, pre, procedures]. WIDAI-only: [act, assessment, categories, conformity, documentation, high].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 77.0% suggests semantic tension between the FDE and matched KSA. The competency domains may be adjacent but not functionally aligned.

Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.172 with low STS 0.552. Candidate selection was likely driven by shared vocabulary rather than functional alignment. The functionally correct KSA may not have been in the top-5 candidates.
